title: Notification fan-out — raised events resolve into recipient batches (rendered from approved templates)
business_reason: SPEC_VS_BUILT_GAP §3.11: notification fan-out is a missing 'nervous system'. Events were raised (school_activated, crm_followup_due, ...) into notification_events but never fanned out to recipients. Build the fan-out: an event -> a notification_batch (rendered from an APPROVED template) -> resolved notification_recipients.

design.pure_helper: server/notifications/fanout.ts resolveRecipients(event, channel) -> recipients (school_user for school/coordinator resolvers, staff_user for ops/manager; never fabricates when school_id missing). Unit-tested.
design.fanout: fanoutEvent(supabase, eventId, actor): resolve an ACTIVE template by event_code; if none -> SUPPRESS the event + audit (config gap surfaced, NEVER bypass the required-template control); else create a notification_batch (template_id set) + insert notification_recipients + mark the event queued + audit. drainPendingEvents drains all 'created' events.

schema_changes.0025: MISTAKE — relaxed notification_batches.template_id NOT NULL to avoid seeding a template. Reverted.
schema_changes.0026: Reverts 0025 — restores template_id NOT NULL (a required template is a legitimate control; the fan-out RESOLVES a template).
schema_changes.0027: Drops the leftover single-column UNIQUE(recipient_key) on notification_recipients (from 0001) — it blocked a recipient from being in more than one batch; the composite (batch_id, recipient_key) from 0023 is the real natural key. Founder-approved.
schema_changes.0028: Completes the 0023 sweep — drops 4 more leftover single-column uniques that shadow their (key/code, version) composites (setting_definitions/versions.setting_key, report_definitions.report_code, exam_material_templates.template_code), which had been breaking versioning. Founder-approved.
schema_changes.guardrail: check_unique_constraints.py strengthened: also flags a single-column unique whose column is part of a composite unique on the same table (catches the *_key/*_code shadow case the *_id/*_version suffix rule missed); regex made whitespace-tolerant.

param_14.question: How does a raised event become recipient notifications?
param_14.answer: A drain endpoint processes 'created' notification_events: each resolves an ACTIVE template for its event_code, creates a notification_batch (which MUST reference a template), resolves recipients into notification_recipients, and marks the event queued. An event with no active template is SUPPRESSED and surfaced (audited), never bypassed — the template control is honored.
param_14.security_implications: required-template control preserved (governed messaging, no bypass); recipient resolution is server-side; consent/suppression honored (no-template -> suppressed). Found + fixed leftover single-column uniques that broke one-to-many (recipient) and versioning (settings/reports/materials).
param_14.approval_implications: event batches are created in 'queued' state from approved templates; actual send/delivery + dry-run approval remain in notification_ops lifecycle.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: delivery/dispatch (server/notifications/delivery.ts) consumes queued batches; per-recipient channel address resolution (email/SMS) beyond in-app routing is a follow-up; auto-trigger fan-out on event raise (vs the drain endpoint).
param_14.assumptions: templates carry event_code + status active; recipient_resolver strings map to school/staff audiences.
